golden_categories.name: Standard FQDN Validation
golden_categories.description: Validates fully qualified domain names (FQDN) with default options.

golden_categories.name: Trailing Dot Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow a trailing dot.

golden_categories.name: No TLD Requirement
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs without requiring a top-level domain (TLD).

golden_categories.name: Numeric TLDs Without Requirement
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with numeric TLDs when TLD requirement is disabled.

golden_categories.name: Wildcard Option
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with the option to allow wildcard domains.

golden_categories.name: Combined Options
golden_categories.description: Validates FQDNs with multiple options enabled: trailing dot, underscores, and numeric TLDs.

generated_categories.name: Default and Basic Validation
generated_categories.description: Covers the default behavior of the validator and basic validation rules, such as rejecting invalid FQDNs and ensuring compliance with standard domain name rules.

generated_categories.name: TLD Validation Options
generated_categories.description: Focuses on tests related to TLD validation, including allowing or disallowing TLDs, numeric TLDs, and whitespace in TLDs.

generated_categories.name: Domain Part Validation Options
generated_categories.description: Covers tests for domain part-specific options, such as allowing underscores, trailing dots, and wildcards, as well as rejecting overly long domain parts.

generated_categories.name: Length Constraints
generated_categories.description: Tests the behavior of the validator when max length constraints are enforced or ignored.

golden_to_generated.reason: GENC-1 aligns with GC-1 as both focus on standard FQDN validation with default options, including rejecting invalid FQDNs and ensuring compliance with standard domain name rules.
GENC-3 includes validation for trailing dots, which aligns with GC-2, but also covers other domain part-specific options not mentioned in GC-2.
GENC-2 partially aligns with GC-3 as it includes tests for TLD validation options, including allowing or disallowing TLDs, but also includes unrelated tests for whitespace in TLDs.
GENC-2 partially aligns with GC-4 as it includes tests for numeric TLDs when TLD requirement is disabled, but also includes other TLD-related tests not specific to numeric TLDs.
GENC-3 partially aligns with GC-5 as it includes validation for wildcard domains, but also covers other domain part-specific options not mentioned in GC-5.
GENC-3 partially aligns with GC-6 as it includes validation for trailing dots, underscores, and other domain part-specific options, but does not explicitly mention the combination of these options.

generated_to_golden.reason: GENC-1 aligns with GC-1 as both focus on standard FQDN validation with default options, including rejecting invalid FQDNs and ensuring compliance with standard domain name rules.
GENC-2 spans GC-3 and GC-4 as it includes tests for TLD validation options, including allowing or disallowing TLDs and numeric TLDs, but also includes unrelated tests for whitespace in TLDs.
GENC-3 spans GC-2, GC-5, and GC-6 as it includes validation for trailing dots, wildcard domains, and underscores, but does not explicitly focus on the combination of these options as in GC-6.
GENC-4 focuses on length constraints, which is not covered by any golden category.

novel_generated_categories.reason: GENC-4 introduces a novel focus on length constraints, which is not addressed by any of the golden categories.
